steps.title: Fastest check: compare a table or graph
steps.content: For an equivalent-expression question, enter the original expression and the answer choices in a calculator table or graph. The correct choice must match for several inputs, not just one. The matching choice is A, $y=7\left(x-\frac{b}{14}\right)^{2}-6-\frac{b^{2}}{28}$.

steps.title: Algebra check
steps.content: Use the work below to confirm the identity for every allowed input. This matters because one random matching input is only a quick screen, not a proof that two expressions are always equivalent.

steps.title: Pick a convenient positive value for b
steps.content: The answer must work for any positive constant $b$, so test a simple legal value such as $b=14$. The original becomes $y=7x^2-14x-6$.

steps.title: Use table values to test choices
steps.content: Evaluate the original and each choice on the same $x$-list. The list for the correct answer is identical to the original list; the other choices differ.

steps.title: Confirm the choice
steps.content: The expression in A matches the original expression across the tested values. That identifies the equivalent form without completing the square by hand, so A is correct.
